title: Move lexicon JSON into corpus crate
description: Move the canonical com.superbfowle.tass.* lexicon JSON files into crates/tass-lex-corpus so the corpus crate physically owns the data. Keep the crate data-first: no build.rs, no generated structs, just embedded JSON metadata/accessors and a crate-local lexicons/ directory that codegen can consume.
acceptance_criteria: Lexicon JSON files live under crates/tass-lex-corpus/lexicons.; tass-lex-corpus embeds crate-local files and exposes useful metadata for each document.; tassle-codegen defaults to the corpus crate lexicons directory.; README/docs no longer point at a removed root lexicons/ directory.; cargo test -p tass-lex-corpus and root codegen smoke test pass.
notes: Moved canonical com.superbfowle.tass.* JSON files from root lexicons/ into crates/tass-lex-corpus/lexicons. Updated tass-lex-corpus to embed crate-local files and expose file_name plus LEXICON_DIR metadata. Updated tassle-codegen default input to crates/tass-lex-corpus/lexicons and fixed local docs links.
close_reason: Corpus crate now physically owns the lexicon JSON files and codegen consumes that path by default.

title: Move Rust workspace to repository root
description: Make this repository a normal multi-crate Rust project from the repo root, instead of requiring commands to be run from crates/. Add or move workspace metadata so cargo commands work from the top-level checkout. Tackle tass-lex-corpus while doing this so lexicon JSON has a data-first package boundary in the workspace.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p tassle-cli or renamed CLI package works from the repository root.; The Rust workspace includes the lexicon corpus crate.; Docs/examples no longer require cd crates for normal Rust commands.; Existing unrelated files are left untouched.; The work is committed in logical jj commits.
notes: Implemented root Cargo workspace. Moved workspace metadata from crates/Cargo.toml to repository-root Cargo.toml, moved Cargo.lock to the root, added root target ignores, and updated README/codegen examples so cargo commands run from the repository root. Included existing tassle-ledger member and new tass-lex-corpus member.
close_reason: Root multi-crate Rust workspace is in place and verified.

title: Remove legacy Mage stats compatibility
description: Remove old legacy Mage compatibility from the Rust CLI/model path now that actor.rpg.stats/mage has been updated to the current lowercase per-system payload. Keeping self.mage fallback, capitalized field aliases, Force->forces, and scalar Willpower support is confusing and masks schema/model problems. Future Mage work should target the current rpg.actor mageStats shape only.
acceptance_criteria: mage stats reads actor.rpg.stats/mage as the canonical source and does not fall back to actor.rpg.stats/self.mage.; Capitalized field aliases such as Arete, Prime, Force, and scalar Willpower are removed from the main normalizer.; Structured willpower.permanent/temporary remains supported.; Help/docs no longer describe legacy fallback as normal behavior.; Tests or smoke output confirm the current lowercase mage record still works.

title: Add profile config-backed auth login stub
description:
Add a Rust-side `auth login` command that does not perform OAuth yet. It resolves a handle/DID through Jacquard, writes a durable profile config under XDG config, and makes that profile the default actor for read commands.

## Direction

- Canonical command: `tassle auth login <did-or-handle>`.
- This is profile login, not token auth yet.
- It must not destroy existing profiles.
- Store profiles as separate TOML snippets under `config.toml.d/`, one file per profile primary key / DID.
- Use the active/default profile for commands whose `--repo`/`--actor` is omitted.
- This is the foundation for later OAuth login and multi-profile support.

## ADR content to capture

- Why profile config exists before OAuth.
- Why config is a directory of TOML fragments instead of a single mutable config file.
- Filename/key strategy for DIDs.
- How active/default profile is represented without destroying others.
- Future figments-rs `.d` expansion integration.
notes: Implemented profile-login stub: `tassle auth login <did-or-handle>` resolves DID/PDS via Jacquard, writes `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-~/.config}/tassle/config.toml.d/<did>.toml`, preserves existing unrelated TOML content, and lets read commands default `--repo`/`--actor` from the newest active profile. ADR written at `doc/adr/0001-profile-config-before-auth.md`.
close_reason: Done: auth login profile stub, default actor config, and ADR implemented.

title: Add Rust mage stats command using actor.rpg.stats/mage
description:
Add a Rust clap command group `tassle mage` with initial subcommand `stats` that reads Mage: The Ascension sheet data from `actor.rpg.stats` using Jacquard XRPC.

## Context

Live record inventory for `jauntywk.bsky.social` / `did:plc:zjbq26wybii5ojoypkso2mso` found both shapes:

- `actor.rpg.stats/mage` exists and uses the per-system envelope shape: `{ system: "mage", data: { ... } }`.
- `actor.rpg.stats/self` also exists and has legacy inline blocks: `{ mage: { ... }, reverie: { ... }, ... }`.
- The `mage` data in both records was byte-for-byte identical at the time of inspection.
- Existing real Mage fields are legacy/capitalized: `Arete`, `Willpower`, `Prime`, `Force` singular, etc.; `quintessence` and `paradox` were absent.

## Implementation direction

- Command shape: `tassle mage stats`.
- Flags should include at least:
  - `--actor <did-or-handle>` / `-a` for target actor; default to dev reference or active account once auth exists.
  - `--rkey <rkey>` for explicit override/debugging.
  - `--json` for raw/normalized JSON.
- Default lookup order:
  1. `actor.rpg.stats/mage` and normalize `{ system, data }`.
  2. fall back to `actor.rpg.stats/self` and normalize inline `mage`.
- Normalize legacy field spelling/case for display: `Force` -> `forces`, Capitalized sphere names -> lowercase keys.
- Show actual source URI/rkey/shape in the output so users can see whether they are reading canonical `/mage` or fallback `/self`.

## XRPC sketch

CLI reads map directly to ATProto repo queries for now:

- Resolve actor handle -> DID/PDS using Jacquard identity resolver.
- GET `com.atproto.repo.getRecord`:
  - `repo`: target DID
  - `collection`: `actor.rpg.stats`
  - `rkey`: `mage`
- On `RecordNotFound`, GET same collection with `rkey`: `self`.

Future server-side equivalent could become an appview query:

- `com.superbfowle.tass.getMageStats`
  - query params: `actor`, optional `campaign`, optional `rkey`, optional `includeRaw`
  - output: normalized mage stats plus `source` metadata (`uri`, `cid`, `shape`, `updatedAt`)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo run -p tassle-cli -- mage stats --actor jauntywk.bsky.social` fetches the live `actor.rpg.stats/mage` record through Jacquard XRPC.
- [ ] Output includes Arete, Willpower, and all nine spheres from the live record, including handling `Force` singular.
- [ ] Missing `quintessence`/`paradox` is displayed as absent rather than zero.
- [ ] `--json` prints normalized data and source metadata.
- [ ] Fallback to `self.mage` works when `/mage` is missing.
notes:
Correction: the intended command is `tassle mage stats`; initial create command lost the backticked text because zsh evaluated it in the ticket title. Also prefer Jacquard public client/session APIs for reads: use `jacquard::client::BasicClient` / `XrpcClient::send(ListRecords)` / `AgentSessionExt::get_record` where available. Avoid a direct `reqwest` dependency in tassle-cli unless Jacquard lacks the needed operation. Direct `tokio` runtime dependency is still expected for async CLI execution.
Implemented first Rust slice: `tassle mage stats` and alias `tassle mage list` read `actor.rpg.stats/mage` first, normalize the per-system envelope, and fall back to `self.mage` if needed. Output treats `actor.rpg.stats` as the player state/history anchor and reports missing quintessence/paradox as absent.
close_reason: Done: mage stats/list implemented and verified against live actor.rpg.stats/mage.

title: Make the Rust Jacquard CLI the primary product path
description:
Rebase tassle's active CLI direction onto the Rust workspace (`crates/tassle-cli`) using clap v4 derive and Jacquard as the atproto/XRPC toolkit.

## Context

The repo already has a Rust workspace with generated Jacquard lexicon types and a small clap CLI skeleton. The previous direction treated Rust as schema/codegen support and TypeScript as product path; that is no longer desired. The Rust CLI should become the main path.

Relevant current files:

- `crates/tassle-cli/src/main.rs` — clap derive CLI skeleton
- `crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/generate/node.rs` — existing generated Node record command
- `crates/tassle-lexicons/` — Jacquard-generated `com.superbfowle.tass.*` types
- `doc/jacquard-use.md` — older note that must be revised because it says not to migrate the TS CLI

## Design direction

- Use clap v4 derive for all command structure.
- Use Jacquard for atproto primitives and XRPC; avoid reimplementing transport/auth.
- For HTTP, align with Jacquard's stack: `reqwest 0.12` via `jacquard-common::http_client::HttpClient` and `XrpcExt`, plus `tokio` runtime.
- Prefer Jacquard bootstrap XRPC request types for `com.atproto.repo.{listRecords,getRecord,createRecord,putRecord,deleteRecord}`.
- Keep generated `tassle-lexicons` types as canonical record builders/validators.
- Use `jiff` for new project-owned time code where possible, but Jacquard currently exposes `chrono`-based `Datetime`; do not fight generated types unnecessarily.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/tassle-cli` has the dependencies needed for async XRPC: `jacquard`, `reqwest`, `tokio` or equivalent through Jacquard.
- [ ] `tassle` Rust binary can perform at least one public XRPC read through Jacquard/reqwest, not raw curl.
- [ ] README/development docs identify the Rust CLI as primary and TS CLI as legacy/reference or remove stale TS-primary wording.
- [ ] `doc/jacquard-use.md` is updated to remove the old "Do NOT migrate the TS CLI to Rust" recommendation.
- [ ] Existing Rust `generate node`/`samples` functionality still builds.
notes: Transport direction refinement: do not add direct `reqwest` usage/dependency just because XRPC is HTTP. Jacquard already wraps `reqwest` behind `jacquard-common::http_client::HttpClient` and exposes public unauthenticated clients plus XRPC traits. First implementation should try `jacquard::client::BasicClient` and bootstrap request types in `jacquard_common::xrpc::atproto` for list/get/create/put/delete. Add direct `reqwest` only if there is a concrete gap.
close_reason: Done: Rust CLI is primary in docs, tassle-cli uses clap + tokio + Jacquard, and repo list performs live public listRecords through Jacquard without direct reqwest usage.

title: Global --format json|table flag
description: Top-level --format json|table (default table) replacing per-command --json; migrate commands::emit and the auth/config commands to a global format dispatcher. Sets up --format cbor for later.

title: Global --profile <name> flag
description: Top-level clap --profile <name> threaded into config::active_figment so every command honours the per-invocation profile override. Precedence: --profile > TASSLE_PROFILE > config file. Cross-cutting; touches the Cli struct and every command that loads the figment.

title: tassle auth switch
description: Set the active profile by writing profile = <name> to base config.toml (same as config set profile=), validate the fragment exists, no token work. Hidden top-level switch alias.

title: tassle auth logout
description: Revoke (deleteSession via jacquard) and remove the AtpSession row from the profile jac-store-fjall store. Default target = active profile; accept a profile or did arg; --all clears every stored session.

title: tassle auth status / whoami
description: Enumerate every profile (config.toml.d fragments) and, for each, look up its stored AtpSession in the profile jac-store-fjall store, decode the access_jwt exp claim, mark the active profile, show did/handle/pds/storage_path. Honour the global --format. Hidden top-level whoami alias.

title: Real app-password auth login (replace ADR 0001 stub)
description: Replace the non-authenticating ADR 0001 auth login stub with real app-password auth: prompt for identifier+password (or accept flags/env), run CredentialSession resume_or_login (createSession), persist the AtpSession to the profile jac-store-fjall store, set the profile active. Followed by auth status / logout / switch. App-password only for MVP (OAuth deferred). --profile and --format json|table global flags threaded through.
notes:
Login verb landed (auth-store feature). auth login <actor> now does real app-password createSession: resolves --password > TASSLE_PASSWORD > rpassword prompt; opens the active profile's FjallAuth store (explicit storage_path or default config.toml.d/../store/<profile>.fjall); CredentialSession::new + resume(&SessionHint) and on LoginRequired calls login_from_challenge (createSession); jacquard persists the AtpSession JWTs into the fjall store automatically; the non-secret did/handle/pds/session_id land in config.toml.d/<profile>.toml via profile_config::write_value_at. Compile-verified for both feature configs; runtime-verified up to the network call (with a fake handle it reaches jacquard identity resolution and fails there as expected — needs real creds for a full createSession). Without auth-store, falls back to the ADR-0001 profile-only stub. REMAINING (not in this increment): auth status / logout / switch verbs; --profile and --format global flags threaded through account-scoped commands. Real-cred end-to-end verification pending.
Login verb delivered and compile/runtime-verified (reaches jacquard createSession). The remainder of this ticket — status/logout/switch verbs and the --profile/--format/--account globals — has been split into dedicated children: tass-auth-status, tass-auth-logout, tass-auth-switch, tass-cli-profile-flag, tass-cli-format-flag, tass-cli-account-flag. Closing this one for the login verb.

title: Extract crates/tassle-app with Runtime::from_profile bootstrap
description: Create a shared tassle-app library crate holding the domain actions (currently inlined in tassle-cli/src/commands) plus Runtime::from_profile(figment, profile): extract the active figment profile, boot that profile jac-store-fjall store, construct a jacquard CredentialSession, return an Agent. Both tassle-cli and a future web service consume this so auth wiring is neither stranded in the CLI nor wrapped in a confusing layer.

title: Auth spike: fjall store + CredentialSession resume on empty store
description: Throwaway spike proving the stack composes end to end: open a jac-store-fjall AppPasswordStore via its bon builder, construct a jacquard CredentialSession, call resume() against an empty store and observe LoginRequired. Confirms the post-cleanup bon builder surface and the 0.12 trait alignment before real plumbing. Lives in .test-agent/ or an example bin.
notes: Done. crates/tassle-cli/examples/auth_spike.rs opens a FjallAuth (Cbor) via FjallAuth::open(tempdir), wraps CredentialSession::new(Arc<app_password()>, Arc<JacquardResolver::default()>) and calls resume(SessionHint::any()). Compiled and RUN: empty store returns LoginRequired{identifier=None,session_id=None} without touching the network (Any hint short-circuits before resolver). Confirmed post-cleanup API: FjallAuth is the high-level handle with .app_password()/.oauth() accessors sharing one fjall DB; bon builder is FjallAuth::builder().path(p).codec(c).retry(r).harden(h).lock(l).build(). doc/auth-design.md §4.2 updated with the real surface.

title: Add jac-store-fjall as workspace path dependency
description: Add ~/src/jac-store-fjall as a path dependency of the tassle workspace, behind a cargo feature so the base CLI stays lean. Verify the unified build resolves jacquard to a single 0.12.x across both crates so the SessionStore/ClientAuthStore trait impls type-check against the same jacquard. Unblocks the auth spike.
notes: Done. Added jac-store-fjall as a workspace path dep (../jac-store-fjall), feature-gated in tassle-cli behind `auth-store` (optional dep) so the base CLI stays lean. cargo check -p tassle-cli clean both with and without --features auth-store; Cargo.lock unifies jacquard to a single 0.12.1 across tassle + jac-store-fjall (trait impls align). jac-store-fjall's hydrant fjall [patch.crates-io] does not cross the path boundary, so tassle resolves fjall from crates.io — fine for the auth MVP.

title: App-password auth MVP: jac-store-fjall sessions + figment profile switching
description: Land real app-password auth in tassle by composing three libraries rather than a new auth crate: figments-rs (figment2) profiles as the login switcher, jac-store-fjall (fjall backend) as the Jacquard SessionStore, and jacquard 0.12 for CredentialSession/Agent. Bootstrap wiring lives in a shared tassle-app crate consumed by both the CLI and a future web service. Amends ADR 0001: real auth lands as app-password (OAuth deferred). See doc/auth-design.md.

title: Gate corpus samples behind default feature
description: Add a samples feature to tass-lex-corpus, enabled by default, so embedded sample JSON and sample accessors are available normally but can be disabled by minimal consumers.
acceptance_criteria: tass-lex-corpus has default = [samples].; SampleRecord, SAMPLE_DIR, SAMPLES, iter_samples, and get_sample are gated behind the samples feature.; cargo test -p tass-lex-corpus passes with defaults.; cargo check -p tass-lex-corpus --no-default-features passes.; tassle-cli still builds with default corpus features.
notes: Added default-on samples feature to tass-lex-corpus. SampleRecord, SAMPLE_DIR, SAMPLES, iter_samples, get_sample, and sample tests are gated behind feature = samples. Lexicon JSON remains always available. Verified default tests, no-default-features check, and tassle-cli check.
close_reason: Default-on samples feature implemented and verified.

title: Move samples into corpus crate
description: Move generated sample records under crates/tass-lex-corpus so the corpus crate owns both lexicon JSON and canonical example record JSON. Expose samples from the corpus crate and update tassle samples to regenerate that directory by default.
acceptance_criteria: Sample JSON files live under crates/tass-lex-corpus/samples.; tass-lex-corpus embeds sample metadata/accessors.; tassle samples regenerates crates/tass-lex-corpus/samples by default.; README no longer references a root samples/ directory.; cargo test -p tass-lex-corpus and cargo check -p tassle-cli pass.
close_reason: Sample JSON moved into tass-lex-corpus and exposed by the corpus API; CLI default regeneration path updated and verified.

title: Pluggable record-event source trait (hydrant / atproto-tap / jetstream)
description: Define an EventSource trait that yields normalized record events {did, collection, rkey, action, record_body(JSON), cid, live, cursor} so the ledger fold is decoupled from the sync substrate. This is the dual of the LedgerStore trait: source-agnostic in, store-agnostic out. Lets us run multiple implementations side by side and pick what we like.
design: FINDING (hydrant assessment): hydrant DOES write full record bodies into fjall, not just metadata. RecordEvt.record is Arc<RawValue> (full record JSON); live:bool flags backfill(false) vs live(true); records persist to a 'blocks' keyspace and cursor-replay reads them back from disk; XRPC getRecordByUri/getUriRecord return {uri,cid,value}; countRecords exists. vs-tap doc: record events are replayable (global ordered event log + client cursor), identity/account are ephemeral; EPHEMERAL mode keeps a bounded TTL window. CONCLUSION: hydrant is a usable base-data event-source we can project off of WITHOUT fighting it or writing our own projections into it (user-keyspace exists if we ever want to). Candidate impls behind the trait: (1) embedded hydrant (tass-embed-hydrant) — own .data/hydrant/, narrow com.superbfowle.tass.* filter; (2) ngerakines atproto-tap (atproto-crates) — sharded /channel, server-managed cursors, no replay; (3) a jetstream consumer. Keep our own projections in our own LedgerStore regardless of source.

title: Rename Rust crates to tass prefixes
description: Rename current Rust workspace crates/packages from tassle-* to tass-* where they are crate/package prefixes. Target names: tass-cli, tass-lex, tass-codegen, tass-validate. Keep user-facing decisions separate: the binary command may remain tassle unless explicitly renamed later, but crate/package prefixes should not use tassle-* going forward. Update Cargo.toml package names, dependency names/imports, docs, and examples consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Rust workspace package names use tass-* prefixes.; Internal dependency names/import paths are updated.; README and codegen docs no longer instruct users to refer to tassle-* crate package names except historical notes if needed.; cargo check for the workspace passes.; The CLI binary naming decision is documented separately if it remains tassle.

title: Add pure lexicon corpus package
description:
Create a source-of-truth package/crate for lexicon documents themselves, separate from generated Rust bindings. This should expose our authored lexicon JSON in a semi-pure form with minimal or no Rust logic: canonical JSON files, metadata/manifest, reproducible discovery, and optional tiny helpers to iterate embedded documents. Generated crates such as tass-lex and future tass-lex-mage should consume this corpus rather than treating generated Rust as the source.

The goal is to keep lexicons as data first. Rust bindings, validators, docs, and sample generators are downstream artifacts. Use tass-* names everywhere; do not introduce new tassle-* package/crate prefixes.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical com.superbfowle.tass.* lexicon JSON has a dedicated package/corpus boundary.; Generated Rust bindings are clearly downstream of the corpus.; Corpus can be consumed by codegen/tests without copying paths ad hoc.; The package contains minimal Rust, preferably only include_str/include_dir style accessors if it is a crate at all.; README/docs explain source-of-truth versus generated artifacts.; New package/crate names use tass-* prefixes, not tassle-* prefixes.
notes: Implemented initial data-first corpus crate at crates/tass-lex-corpus. It embeds the existing com.superbfowle.tass.* JSON documents via include_str!, exposes deterministic iter()/get() accessors, and has a smoke test covering the six current lexicons. The JSON files remain the source input for now; generated Rust bindings stay downstream.
close_reason: Initial tass-lex-corpus crate added and verified.

title: Clean up tass lexicon codegen
description:
Improve the existing generated Rust binding path for our authored com.superbfowle.tass.* lexicons. Current generated files compile but emit repeated unused import warnings, and validation registration is manual in tassle-validate because generated LexiconSchema impls are not inventory-submitted. This ticket is for our own tass lexicons, separate from third-party rpg.actor generation and Mage-focused modeling.

Naming direction: generated bindings should become tass-lex, with tass-lex-corpus as the source data package. Do not add new tassle-* crate names.
acceptance_criteria: Generated tass-lex bindings compile without repeated unused-import warnings or warnings are intentionally suppressed in generated code.; Validation no longer needs manual inventory registration for every generated schema, or the limitation is documented with a codegen follow-up.; Regeneration command remains documented and reproducible.; No rpg.actor scope is added to this crate by accident.; Names use tass-* prefixes, not tassle-* prefixes.
notes: New prerequisite/design direction: introduce tass-lex-corpus as the data-first source of lexicon JSON. tass-lex should be treated as generated Rust bindings downstream of that corpus, not as the canonical lexicon store.

title: Patch rpg.actor stats lexicon for Jacquard codegen
description:
Make the third-party actor.rpg.stats lexicon usable with Jacquard codegen without forking semantics unnecessarily. Current blockers found by test generation: 12 object schemas without properties in Pathfinder/Starfinder free-form maps, and one string format date at spotemStats.lastDaily. A mechanical preprocessing layer adding properties: {} to free-form object schemas and dropping unsupported format: date allowed full codegen to complete in .test-agent.

This is about full third-party lexicon generation. It is separate from tassle-lex-mage, which can be narrower and should focus on the current Mage payload.
acceptance_criteria: Document the exact rpg.actor stats codegen incompatibilities.; Decide whether to patch upstream copy, preprocess before codegen, or improve Jacquard parser tolerance.; Full actor.rpg.stats codegen succeeds in a reproducible command.; Generated output compiles or compile blockers are recorded.; Do not block the narrow tassle-lex-mage model on full rpg.actor generation.

title: Add tass-lex-mage model for actor.rpg.stats/mage
description:
Create a narrow Mage-focused Rust model for current actor.rpg.stats/mage records. This should use the current per-system rkey envelope and lowercase mageStats payload from rpg.actor. Do not spend effort preserving old capitalized legacy self.mage data; legacy data is not relevant going forward.

The model should cover mageStats, mageVariableStat, and mageWillpower first, then feed mage stats output and future ledger validation context. It may be handwritten initially or generated from a curated/patched Mage-only lexicon subset, but it should not pretend to be the full actor.rpg.stats implementation. The package/crate name should be tass-lex-mage, not tassle-lex-mage.
acceptance_criteria: Typed MageStats model exists with serde support.; actor.rpg.stats/mage per-system envelope can deserialize into the model.; mage stats command uses the model instead of ad-hoc serde_json field reads.; Legacy capitalized self.mage compatibility is not required for the new model.; Willpower permanent/temporary, backgrounds, quintessence, paradox, spheres, and meditation are represented.

title: Ledger fold over hydrant event stream (normalize -> per-DID balances + snapshot/cursor)
description: Wire doc/ledger.md fold onto the embedded hydrant event source. Classify each com.superbfowle.tass.* record (node/meditate/tassilize/enervate) from event.record into a normalized LedgerEvent, fold in createdAt-then-URI order into per-DID NodeBalance/TassBalance/patternQuintessenceDelta, surface anomalies (negative balances, unknown refs, bad timestamps) rather than hiding them.
design: Tallying: store the normalized event log per DID in fjall (durable, ordered) AND a materialized balance snapshot + last-folded cursor. Rebuild snapshot by replay when missing/stale — same as statusphere's 'replay from 0 to rebuild index', but persisted. Gives recomputability (ledger.md's core property) + O(1) reads. Time-based Node regen / half-life ticks (see tass-half-life-ticks) settle lazily on read (lazy-mint-on-touch keeps energy bounded, per design doc open question). Read cmds first: tassle ledger balance|history|inspect <uri>. Reuse the CEL item envelope from ledger.md so filtering matches other list/read cmds. Validation hook (meditate/tassilize/enervate --dry-run, force flag) comes after the fold reads clean.
acceptance_criteria: tassle ledger balance/history/inspect read from folded fjall state; snapshot recomputable from event log replay; anomalies displayed not hidden.

title: Per-DID fjall ledger store (LedgerStore trait, lazy-open keyspace LRU)
description: Derived ledger/service state needs durable storage in fjall. User wants one fjall db (keyspace) per DID: per-DID WAL + locking, independent lifecycle (rm dir to forget a DID), independent backup/snapshot, small corruption blast radius — mirrors atproto's per-repo sovereignty model. Cost: fjall is designed for ONE keyspace with MANY partitions, not many keyspaces; each keyspace has its own journal, memtables, flush/compaction threads, and FDs. Many DIDs = many small files + many FDs.
design: Recommend a LedgerStore trait so the fold doesn't care about physical layout. Two layouts behind it: (A) one keyspace, one partition per DID (or partition-per-collection with DID key prefix) — fewest files, shared journal/fsync, coarser isolation; (B) per-DID keyspace directory — fine-grained locking + snapshot story the user wants. To bound the per-DID-keyspace cost: lazy-open on touch + LRU of open keyspaces (close idle ones) so you never hold 10k keyspaces open at once. Use the SAME fjall version as hydrant (via hydrant's user-keyspace feature reexport, or pin 90-008/fjall rev) to avoid format/symbol conflicts. Data dir layout: .data/ledger/<did>/ per DID -> feeds the btrfs subvolume ticket.
notes: PROGRESS (committed 56d708a): tassle-ledger crate added. LedgerStore trait (append_events/read_events/snapshot/cursor/commit_fold/forget) + FjallLedgerStore. Per-DID fjall 3.1.5 database under <root>/<did-dir>/ (events_by_cursor + meta keyspaces); commit_fold writes events+snapshot+cursor in one durable batch (PersistMode::SyncAll). Open-handle pool = moka 0.12 sync Cache (max_open + time_to_idle, flush-on-evict listener) — chose moka over mini-moka because mini-moka lacks eviction listeners and we need flush-on-evict. fjall 3.1.5 mainline (same major as hydrant's 3.x fork) so co-locating with hydrant's keyspace stays possible later. did_dir() sanitizes reserved chars to '_'. 4 tests pass offline (roundtrip/atomicity/reopen-persist/forget). REMAINING: (1) events_by_created_at secondary index + records_latest/anomalies partitions per doc/ledger.md layout — defer to tass-ledger-fold which owns interpretation; (2) harden the open-collision window with a weak-map so an evicted-but-still-live DID re-opens the same Database; (3) snapshot schema/version byte; (4) evaluate turso/libsql alt behind the same trait.

title: Add 'hydrant' cargo feature + embed Hydrant in-process; tassle listen
description: Add a 'hydrant' cargo feature to tassle-cli that pulls in the hydrant git dep and compiles the listener subcommands (listen/worker/serve/hydrant env|run). Without the feature, tassle stays the lean record-generating CLI. Follow the statusphere example: Config::from_env() (HYDRANT_ prefix), filter to com.superbfowle.tass.* signals + collections, subscribe(cursor), classify event.record like statusphere's handle_stream, advance cursor only after fjall commit. Gate clap subcommands behind #[cfg(feature="hydrant")].
design: hydrant is a git dep (not on crates.io, like jacquard). RISK: jacquard version skew — hydrant uses jacquard-common/api/identity/repo 0.11 (crates.io) while tassle uses jacquard from rsform git default branch. Must reconcile versions or the two jacquard copies will conflict (Did/Tid/types). Resolve before/with this ticket. CLI surface from design doc: tassle hydrant env|run, tassle listen, tassle worker, tassle serve, tassle dev-service.

title: Embedded Hydrant listener + ledger service (port hedystia design to Rust)
description: Port doc/hedystia-listener-design.md from the TS/Bun + Hydrant-sidecar shape into the Rust tassle CLI, embedding Hydrant in-process (statusphere example pattern). A 'hydrant' cargo feature turns the whole listener/worker/serve surface on and off so the base CLI stays a lean record generator. Unifies the doc/ledger.md local fold and the hedystia service onto one embedded event source (hydrant subscribe/cursor + hydrant XRPC reads). Derived per-DID state lands in fjall.
design: Hydrant is a Rust LIBRARY crate (~/archive/ptr.pet/hydrant), embeddable via Hydrant::new(Config) -> filter.set_mode/set_signals/set_collections().apply() -> subscribe(cursor) -> EventStream -> tokio::select!{hydrant.run()?, handle_stream}. Features: indexer (default), indexer_stream (default, gives EventStream), backlinks, jetstream, user-keyspace (own fjall keyspaces in hydrant db, same fjall version, no abstraction). It pins a specific git fjall (90-008/fjall rev, v3.1.2, lz4+zstd) + lsm-tree. EPHEMERAL mode exists (no record storage / TTL). Single hydrant db at HYDRANT_DATABASE_PATH.
notes: Updated doc/hedystia-listener-design.md and doc/ledger.md to make embedded Rust Hydrant the primary path: cargo feature gating, statusphere-style subscribe/run loop, data-root layout, per-DID fjall store tradeoffs, listener cursor commit semantics, and event-log-plus-snapshot tallying.

title: Move auth set toward config set
description:
Decide the CLI ownership boundary between `auth` and `config`.

## Current state

`auth login` is really a profile bootstrap command, and `auth set` currently edits arbitrary keys in the active profile TOML fragment. That works mechanically, but naming is wrong: generic key/value config belongs under `config set`.

## Tentative direction

- `auth login` remains the profile/OAuth entrypoint.
- Generic profile/config mutation becomes `config set <key[=value]>` and `config set -u <key>`.
- `config files` lists loaded config fragments and explains precedence.
- `auth set` should either be removed before release or hidden as an alias.
- CEL-style key lookup should not be snuck into `auth set`; handle it as part of the broader CEL/read-list design.

## Blocked by

`tass-figments-config`, because command shape should match the figments-rs loading model.
notes: Done. Generic config editing now lives under 'tassle config set': config set key=value writes to the active profile's drop-in fragment (config.toml.d/<active>.toml), config set -u key removes, bare 'config set key' reads. The special key 'profile' writes to the base config.toml selector (switches active profile). Reuses path-based dotted-key TOML helpers (profile_config::write_value_at / unset_value_at, now pub(crate)). Round-trips with the figment reader (config get). Verified: set pds/session_id, switch profile, create+edit a new fragment, unset, all read back correctly via figment. 'auth set' retained as the legacy did-keyed fragment editor, marked deprecated in its help; removal/aliasing deferred to a cleanup pass.

title: Adopt figments-rs for config.d loading
description:
Integrate `~/src/figments-rs` for Tassle configuration loading once the config surface stabilizes.

## Context

Current Rust profile config is hand-rolled TOML fragment loading under `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-~/.config}/tassle/config.toml.d/`. This was enough for profile bootstrap, but the desired long-term path is figments-rs `.d` expansion/operator semantics.

## Desired command shape after this lands

- Move generic profile/config editing from `auth set` to `config set`.
- Add `config files` to show every loaded config fragment, in load order, with source path and whether it contributed profile/default values.
- Consider keeping `auth set` as a hidden/deprecated alias only if it has users by then.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Evaluate local `~/src/figments-rs` API and document how `.d` expansion/operators work.
- [ ] Replace hand-rolled config fragment loading with figments-rs where appropriate.
- [ ] Add `tassle config files`.
- [ ] Move or alias `auth set` to `config set` based on the final config API.
notes: Foundation landed. New crates/tassle-cli/src/config.rs + commands/config.rs build the tassle figment using the figments-rs operators exactly as intended: base config.toml (non-nested) supplies the 'profile' selector + flat defaults into the Default profile; select_profile_from_config("profile") records the selection; DropIns::new(dropins).profile_gated().operator::<Toml>() loads the matching config.toml.d/<name>.toml fragment. 'tassle config files' prints the full operation-record audit log; 'config list' enumerates drop-in stems marking the active one; 'config get [key]' extracts the active LoginProfile or a dotted field. Verified end to end with sample profiles (base defaults + per-profile override, TASSLE_PROFILE env switching, profile-gated skip notes). LoginProfile uses snake_case serde keys to match TOML. The old hand-rolled profile_config.rs is kept as a bridge until the auth commands migrate (tass-auth-login / tass-config-command-shape).

title: Add auth set unset flag
description:
Add an unset operation to `tassle auth set`.

## Shape

- `tassle auth set -u <key>` removes a dotted TOML path from the active profile fragment.
- Alias long flag: `--unset`.
- Preserve unrelated TOML content.
- Should be safe for nested tables created by `auth set test.value=42`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `auth set test.value=42` writes the value.
- [ ] `auth set -u test.value` removes it.
- [ ] `auth set test.value` reports unset afterward.
notes: Implemented `tassle auth set -u <key>` / `--unset`. Verified by removing `test.value` from the active profile and reading it back as unset.
close_reason: Done: unset flag implemented and verified.

title: Add auth set for profile config values
description:
Add `tassle auth set` for reading and writing values in the active profile config fragment.

## Shape

- `tassle auth set <key>` reads a value from the active profile.
- `tassle auth set <key=value>` writes a value into the active profile.
- Prefer dotted TOML paths for now, e.g. `display.name=Jane`, `profile.default=true`.
- Preserve existing unrelated TOML content.
- Keep profile config file per DID under `config.toml.d/`.

## CEL note

CEL-style lookup is desirable later, but should be designed across all read/list commands rather than only auth config. First implementation should use simple dotted TOML paths and leave CEL selectors to `tass-mage-cel` / command-shape design.
notes: Implemented `tassle auth set <key>` reads and `tassle auth set <key=value>` writes against the active profile TOML fragment. Uses dotted TOML paths and preserves unrelated TOML content. CEL-style lookup was intentionally deferred to the broader read/list filtering design.
close_reason: Done: auth set read/write for active profile TOML fragment implemented and verified.

title: Split mage commands into logical segments
description:
Refine the Rust `tassle mage` command group into more logical segments before it accumulates too much behavior.

## Context

`mage stats` is the first command, but Mage concerns are broader than a single stats read:

- character stats / rpg.actor sheet state
- quintessence history over time
- tass ledger and balances
- Prime-working actions like tassilize
- eventual CEL filtering/projection over record sets

The first implementation can keep `mage stats`/`mage list` as a thin read, but we should split the surface before adding many flags.

## Questions

- Should raw rpg.actor sheet reads live under `mage stats`, `mage sheet`, or `self stats`?
- Should history/log be `mage history`, `mage ledger`, or top-level `history`?
- Should energy actions stay top-level (`tassilize`, `meditate`, `enervate`) while read views group under `mage`?
- How should CEL filtering apply consistently across `mage list`, `self list`, `repo list`, `nodes`, `history`, and `balance`?

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Propose a command tree for `mage`, `self`, and top-level action commands.
- [ ] Decide which commands are aliases and which are canonical.
- [ ] Capture how CEL flags compose across list/read commands.
- [ ] Keep `tassilize` top-level.
notes: Decision implemented in Rust CLI: mage list is canonical for actor.rpg.stats reads, with an optional rkey and --all for collection listing. mage stats is a clap alias for mage list. self stats/self list remain aggregate inspection for actor.rpg.stats/self. Energy ledger/history stays out of mage stats/list and should live under future ledger commands, with top-level tassilize retained. CEL filtering is deferred to the shared normalized list/read envelope work documented in doc/ledger.md.
close_reason: Implemented command shape: mage list canonical, mage stats alias, actor.rpg.stats rkey listing supported; docs updated.

title: Track WIP XRPC shapes alongside CLI commands
description:
Maintain a parallel XRPC/API design track as Rust CLI commands are added, so the command surface can evolve into an appview/service API without later redesign.

## Context

The immediate product is CLI-first, but each CLI command should be designed with its future XRPC equivalent in mind. This is especially important for `mage stats`, `balance`, `history`, `nodes`, and `tassilize`, because those are likely to become public read queries or write procedures in an appview/reality service.

## Working rule

For every new user-visible CLI command, capture a WIP XRPC sketch in either:

- the command's implementation ticket, or
- a dedicated child ticket if the shape is non-trivial.

The sketch should include:

- Query vs procedure.
- NSID candidate.
- Inputs/params.
- Output shape.
- Whether it reads raw PDS records, an indexed projection, or both.
- Which CLI flags map to XRPC params.
- What remains local-only CLI behavior.

## Initial candidate shapes

### `tassle mage stats`

Candidate query: `com.superbfowle.tass.getMageStats`

- params: `actor`, optional `rkey`, optional `includeRaw`
- output: normalized mage stats, source URI/CID/rkey/shape, missing-field list
- backing read: `com.atproto.repo.getRecord` for `actor.rpg.stats/mage`, fallback `self`

### `tassle balance`

Candidate query: `com.superbfowle.tass.getBalance`

- params: `actor`, optional `campaign`, optional `since`, optional `includeAnomalies`
- output: node balances, tass balances, pattern quintessence delta, anomalies
- backing read initially: list current actor's `com.superbfowle.tass.{node,tassilize,meditate,enervate}` records
- future: indexed appview projection

### `tassle history`

Candidate query: `com.superbfowle.tass.getHistory`

- params: `actor`, optional `campaign`, optional `cursor`, optional `limit`, optional `collection[]`
- output: ordered ledger events with source AT-URI/CID and normalized amount/source/target/purpose

### `tassle tassilize`

Candidate procedure: `com.superbfowle.tass.tassilize` or appview helper around record creation

- keep CLI command top-level because tassilization can be initiated by a person with Prime 2 converting their own quintessence into tass, or by/at a Node forming tass.
- params need to distinguish source kind: `source = magePattern | node`, plus source ref when applicable.
- output should include proposed record, derived balance impacts, and published AT-URI if written.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New CLI tickets include WIP XRPC sketches.
- [ ] `mage stats`, `balance`, `history`, and `tassilize` have first-pass query/procedure shapes captured.
- [ ] Any eventual lexicon files for XRPC methods are generated/validated through the Rust/Jacquard path, not hand-waved separately.
notes: Naming correction: use existing `tassilize` spelling for the action/collection/CLI command. User typo was `tasslize`; do not rename the command. Keep `tassilize` top-level because source can be mage-pattern Prime 2 or Node formation.

title: Half-life based emission tick system
description: Design and implement a half-life based tick system for probabilistic emission of records such as node quintessence or tass records. The system should model when an eligible source may next emit by using a half-life parameter instead of a fixed interval. Initial target: a 7 hour half-life, meaning a 50% chance that a new quintessence charge has occurred after 7 hours of eligibility.
design: Use a hazard/decay model where elapsed time determines emission probability: p(dt) = 1 - 2^(-dt / half_life). For a continuous exponential process, lambda = ln(2) / half_life, so a 7 hour half-life yields a mean wait of about 10.1 hours and an average production rate of ln(2) / 7 ~= 0.099 emissions/hour, or about 2.38 emissions/day per continuously eligible source. If the implementation instead checks only at fixed 7 hour intervals with p=0.5 per check, the mean wait is 14 hours, so the ticket should explicitly choose the continuous elapsed-time model or document why discrete checks are intended.
acceptance_criteria: Eligible sources can compute emission probability from elapsed time and configured half-life.; The initial default half-life for quintessence charging is 7 hours.; Documentation or tests state the implied average emission rate: about one emission every 10.1 hours, 2.38/day, for a continuous 7 hour half-life model.; The implementation avoids fixed-interval-only semantics unless intentionally selected and documented.; The tick system can be applied to node quintessence and tass-like record emissions without duplicating rate logic.

title: Global --account <did|handle> flag
description: Top-level --account overriding the profile DID for one call; selects a specific session_id when multiple logins exist for one account. Lower priority (P3) — matters mainly for multi-login-per-account.

title: Add ordinal and range selectors for read results
description:
Add post-filter result selectors for read/list commands. Selectors operate after CEL filtering and before display, so users can pick rows by position without writing field predicates.

## Selector model

- 1-based positive ordinals: 1 is the first result.
- Negative ordinals address from the end: -1 is the last result, -2 is second-last.
- Commas combine selectors: 1,4,5.
- Inclusive ranges pick spans: 2-4.
- Negative ranges are allowed with double hyphen syntax where needed: -2--1.
- Preserve result ordering and avoid duplicate output if selectors overlap.

## Relationship to CEL

CEL answers what records match by content. Selectors answer which positions to show from the matched ordered stream. This should compose with repo, mage, self, and future ledger commands.
acceptance_criteria: Read commands accept a selector flag, likely --pick or --select-position.; Selector grammar supports positive ordinals, negative ordinals, comma lists, and inclusive ranges.; Selectors run after CEL filters and before formatting.; Invalid selectors produce clear diagnostics.; Tests cover 1, -1, 1,3, 2-4, -2--1, and overlapping selectors.

title: btrfs subvolume support for per-DID data dirs
description: If each DID's ledger state lives in its own directory (.data/ledger/<did>/, see per-DID fjall store ticket), make each per-DID dir a btrfs subvolume when the data root is on btrfs. Pays off: cheap O(1) snapshots per actor, btrfs send/receive for backup/replication, per-DID quota, reflink/CoW. Detect btrfs at the data root; create subvolume (btrfs subvolume create) instead of mkdir when available, else fall back to a plain dir. Cleanly delete a DID via subvolume delete. Gate behind capability detection so non-btrfs hosts still work.
notes: Spun out of the embedded-hydrant/per-DID-fjall spitball session. Connects directly to the per-DID keyspace layout decision — only meaningful if we go with per-DID directories rather than one shared keyspace.

title: Add CEL filters and projections to read envelopes
description:
Support Common Expression Language (CEL) predicates and projections over the stable read/list envelope emitted by repo, mage, self, and future ledger commands.

Reference implementation: kube-rs/kube-cel. Tassle is now Rust-first, so evaluate direct Rust integration before considering JS/WASM fallback paths.

## Motivation

Read commands should not grow bespoke flags for every query shape. A single CEL expression model can filter actor.rpg.stats records, Mage-normalized values, repo envelopes, and future ledger rows.

## Command model

- repo is the canonical public record access surface.
- mage preconfigures and normalizes Mage-specific actor.rpg.stats data.
- ledger will expose derived balance/history rows.
- All three should feed CEL the same envelope shape before display.

## Envelope

CEL should see fields like uri, cid, repo, collection, rkey, value, and normalized. Domain commands may add normalized data while preserving the source record value.

## Questions

- Which subset of kube-cel is practical for v1?
- How should CEL handle JSON null/missing values?
- Do value expressions print a projection, while booleans filter rows?
- Can exact rkey predicates be optimized to getRecord internally while preserving list semantics?

## Non-goals

- Do not make CEL a mage-only feature.
- Do not introduce a separate sheet command or obscure actor.rpg.stats as the source term.
- Do not treat CBOR as raw read output; CBOR is for generated/publishable records.
acceptance_criteria: CEL flags apply to repo list and mage stats/list over a shared envelope.; Boolean CEL expressions filter rows or gate single-result output.; Non-boolean CEL expressions can project/print computed values.; kube-rs/kube-cel is evaluated as the Rust-first implementation path.; README command model examples remain aligned with implemented flag names.
notes: Broaden CEL scope beyond `mage stats`: CEL filtering/projection should apply consistently to all read/list commands (`repo list`, `self list`, `mage list`, future `nodes`, `history`, `balance`). Candidate model: each command produces a normalized item envelope `{ uri, cid, collection, rkey, source, value, normalized }`; CEL predicate flags filter item streams, and optional projection expressions compute displayed fields. `kube-rs/kube-cel` remains the semantic reference.
